headings: Section 35. Default; Termination; Termination for Convenience.
text: Section  36. Lien-Free  Project;  Indemnity  from  Liens  and  Claims;  Patents.  Subcontractor  shall  defend, indemnify and hold harmless Contractor and Owner from any and all laborer's, materialmen's, and mechanic's liens upon the improvements, property and premises on which the Subcontractor Work is being performed that arise out of such work, and shall keep such property free and clear of all liens, claims and encumbrances arising out of the performance of this Subcontract. Subcontractor shall defend, indemnify and hold harmless Contractor and its surety from  any  claims  or  causes  of  action  asserted  against  Contractor's  surety  bonds  issued  in  connection  with  the Project. Subcontractor shall defend, indemnify and hold harmless Contractor and Owner from any claims or suits for infringement of patents or violation of patent rights by Subcontractor. If Subcontractor fails to do any of the foregoing, Contractor may withhold from sums then or thereafter due Subcontractor under this Subcontract or any other  agreement  such  amounts  as  Contractor  may  deem  appropriate  to  defend,  indemnify  and  hold  harmless Contractor and Owner from such liens, claim, encumbrances and suits, and may offset any and all damages, loss, costs and expenses incurred, including but not limited to attorneys' fees, arising out of or resulting from such liens, claim, encumbrances and suits, against all amounts withheld and all other sums then or thereafter due Subcontractor under this Subcontract or any other agreement.
